title: Western blotting
steps: Run your protein samples on SDS-PAGE as usual
Soak 6 pieces of Whatman blotting paper, nitrocellulose membrane (all a bit bigger than your gel) and your SDS-PAGE gel in transfer buffer for a few minutes
Assemble the blot: 3 layers of Whatman paper, nitrocellulose membrane (to the positive pole of the device; in the Bio-Rad blotting machine to the bottom), SDS-PAGE gel, 3 layers of Whatman paper
Roll out all possible air bubbles with a tube or some other device
Start blotting: with the Bio-Rad machine either 30 min programme, or TURBO (TURBO might not be always the best option as not all proteins will transfer)
Blocking : Put nitrocellulose membrane in to box and add blocking solution, let it incubate 1,5 h, RT, at shaking

Primary antibody: Add primary antibody solution, incubation recommended O/N, shaking, +4 °C. Can also be done at RT for 1-2 h, but less specific binding and some antibodies might degrade at RT. Collect primary antibody solution, it can be used about 10 times, add 1 % NaN3 and store in +4 °C.
Washing: Rinse twice with PBS-T, and wash with PBS-T 5 min at shaking three times
Secondary antibody: Add secondary antibody solution, incubate 1 h, RT, shaking
Place the membrane on a plastic sheet and pipet the detection solution on the membrane
Fold the plastic and let the solution react at least 1 min

Within the next hour, image with Fujifilm LAS 3000 imager. A 10 sec exposure x 3 should give good images. Remember to take a digitized version without moving the membrane, so you can easily have the molecular weight standard in your image. Exposure time here should be 1/60 sec or 1/100 sec.
creator: Snehadri Sinha
